name: Trifle
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the fruit layer: Cut the sponge cake into very thick slices and spread each piece with a generous amount of raspberry jam.
2. Arrange the cake slices on the bottom of a large glass bowl. If serving individually, divide the cake equally among dishes.
3. Sprinkle almonds over the cake slices.
4. Pour brandy and sherry over the cake and allow it to soak for about 40 minutes (do not let it soak longer as the cake will become soggy).
5. Anne's note: I prefer it soaked overnight, but you can adjust according to taste.
6. In a double boiler, bring milk and cream with vanilla pod to a boil, then remove from heat and discard the vanilla pod.
7. In a separate bowl, mix cornstarch with sugar and eggs. Gradually pour in the milk and cream mixture while stirring continuously.
8. Transfer the custard back to the double boiler and stir until it thickens and becomes creamy. Do not allow it to boil.
9. Let the custard cool slightly, then pour it over the cake slices.
10. Allow the cake to set, then refrigerate until fully chilled.
11. Spread any remaining raspberry jam on top, then cover with whipped cream.
12. Decorate the trifle with citron slices and crystallized violets or strawberry slices before serving (serves 6-8).
